_comment: Updated annotation — QUADAS2 checklist for Xpert MTB/RIF diagnostic accuracy study

article.title: Diagnostic Accuracy and Turnaround Time of the Xpert MTB/RIF Assay in Routine Clinical Practice
article.authors: Kwak N, Choi SM, Lee J, Park YS, Lee C-H, et al.

study_type.notes: Retrospective diagnostic accuracy study evaluating Xpert MTB/RIF assay for tuberculosis and rifampicin resistance detection in routine clinical practice. Cross-sectional design comparing index test to gold standard reference (culture).

1.1.notes: Consecutive patient enrollment: 'retrospectively reviewed the medical records of patients in whom Xpert MTB/RIF assay using sputum were requested'; all consecutive Xpert requests during study period included; n=681 total

1.2.notes: Case-control design avoided: cross-sectional retrospective design; consecutive enrollment of suspected TB patients requesting Xpert; not case-control matching

1.3.notes: Inappropriate exclusions avoided: study included all patients with Xpert requests during study period (Jan 1 2011-May 31 2013); eligibility criteria: sputum Xpert assay requested

1.risk.notes: Overall bias risk for patient selection: LOW - consecutive enrollment of patients meeting clinical suspicion for TB, no selective exclusions, representative of routine clinical practice

2.1.notes: Index test interpretation blinding: retrospective design means Xpert results likely known when gold standard performed; no explicit statement of blinding for interpretation

2.2.notes: Threshold pre-specified: Xpert MTB/RIF uses manufacturer-established thresholds; automated positive/negative determination per manufacturer protocol; no data-driven threshold selection

2.concern.notes: Index test applicability: Xpert MTB/RIF is WHO-endorsed, commercially available point-of-care test; evaluated in routine clinical practice; applicable to clinical TB diagnosis

3.1.notes: Reference standard validity: mycobacterial culture is gold standard for TB diagnosis; DST (drug susceptibility testing) gold standard for rifampicin resistance; both established standards

3.2.notes: Reference standard interpretation blinding: no explicit statement that culture/DST interpretation blinded to Xpert results; likely performed without knowledge but not explicitly stated

3.concern.notes: Reference standard applicability: culture and DST are clinical standards for TB diagnosis; appropriate reference standards for evaluating Xpert in routine practice

4.1.notes: Appropriate interval between tests: Xpert performed within 24 hours of sample collection; culture conducted on same samples; appropriate timing (same day or within 24h)

4.2.notes: Uniform reference standard: all patients received mycobacterial culture; DST applied to culture-positive isolates uniformly; same reference standard applied to all

4.3.notes: All patients included in analysis: 681 total enrolled; all included in TB diagnosis analysis; exclusion of culture-negative/Xpert-positive cases noted for sensitivity purposes but intent-to-diagnose approach

4.concern.notes: Flow and timing applicability: median TAT 0 (0-1) days for Xpert report; 6 (3-7) days for physician confirmation; clinically relevant timing for TB diagnosis decision-making
